The inquest revealed she had been defrauded over five years by scammers from the UK, Germany, the US, and Ghana.
Her tragic death underscores the dangers of scams and the lengths victims may go to reclaim their lost funds.

stories.title: OnePlus Watch 4 officially brings Wear OS 6 in a titanium body, mysterious launch plans [Gallery]
bullets: OnePlus has unveiled the Watch 4, featuring Wear OS 6 in a titanium body, although official launch plans remain unclear.
Details about the smartwatch were posted on a global OnePlus website, indicating a potential shift in the brand's strategy.
The introduction of this device could impact the competitive landscape of wearables, especially in the premium segment.

stories.title: This Frickin’ Guy
bullets: Brandon Hagel scored a career-high 36 goals for the Tampa Bay Lightning during the 2025-2026 NHL season, showcasing his offensive prowess.
Hagel's performance follows a breakout year with 90 points, stepping up after Steven Stamkos' departure and Brayden Point's injury.
His contributions are crucial for the Lightning's playoff aspirations, highlighting the importance of depth in their roster.

stories.title: Gene therapy improves hearing in 90% of patients with inherited deafness in largest trial of its kind
bullets: A groundbreaking gene therapy trial in China improved hearing in 90% of 38 patients with inherited deafness caused by OTOF mutations.
This trial marks the largest of its kind, demonstrating significant advancements in treating genetic hearing loss with a promising success rate.
The results could pave the way for broader applications of gene therapy in auditory health, transforming treatment options for deafness.

stories.title: More than 50% of children in Argentina are poor, says UCA report
bullets: A report from the Catholic University of Argentina revealed that 53.6% of children in Argentina were living in poverty by the end of 2025.
This alarming statistic highlights the ongoing economic struggles, with over 5 million children affected by poverty in the country.
The findings call for urgent policy interventions to address child poverty and improve living conditions in Argentina.

stories.title: We need more radioactive drugs. Can we make them from nuclear waste?
bullets: The demand for radioactive drugs is rising due to new radiotherapies, prompting companies to explore refining them from nuclear waste.
This approach could provide a sustainable source of radioactive materials, addressing shortages and enhancing cancer treatment options.
Innovations in this field may lead to breakthroughs in medical therapies, improving patient outcomes and resource management.

stories.title: a raised walkway weaves tallinn’s museum quarter into the urban fabric
bullets: Georges Batzios Architects proposed a raised walkway that integrates Tallinn City Museum with the urban landscape in Estonia.
This design aims to create a continuous public space, enhancing accessibility and connecting the museum to the city's cultural heritage.
The project emphasizes the importance of urban design in fostering community engagement and cultural appreciation.

stories.title: Table tennis-playing robot on track to becoming world champion
bullets: Sony AI's table tennis-playing robot is rapidly advancing, showing potential to compete against the world's top players.
The robot's learning capabilities are impressive, as it adapts strategies and techniques to improve its game performance significantly.
If successful, this technology could revolutionize sports training and entertainment, merging AI with athletic competition.

stories.title: Want to Read More? Try These 5 Tips
bullets: In honor of World Book Day, Mental Floss shared five tips to encourage reading more books and rekindle a love for literature.
The tips include setting reading goals, joining book clubs, and exploring diverse genres to enhance the reading experience.
These strategies aim to promote literacy and foster a community of readers, emphasizing the importance of books in personal growth.

stories.title: New York Bans Government Employees from Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
bullets: New York's executive order prohibits state employees from insider trading on prediction markets, aiming to ensure ethical governance.
This regulation addresses concerns about conflicts of interest and the misuse of privileged information for personal gain.
The move reflects a growing trend towards transparency and accountability in government operations, reinforcing public trust.

stories.title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
bullets: North Korean hackers utilized AI tools to enhance their cyber operations, reportedly stealing up to $12 million in three months.
Their tactics included creating fake websites and sophisticated malware, showcasing the evolving nature of cybercrime.
This development raises concerns about cybersecurity and the need for stronger defenses against increasingly sophisticated hacking techniques.

stories.title: Hill Road Flyover in Hong Kong
bullets: The Hill Road Flyover in Hong Kong, completed in 1981, was designed to alleviate traffic congestion in Shek Tong Tsui.
Spanning 650 meters, the flyover provides a bypass from Pok Fu Lam Road to Connaught Road West, elevating traffic above street level.
This infrastructure project has significantly improved traffic flow, demonstrating the impact of urban planning on city mobility.

stories.title: The Edition XV headphone: Sounds warm, looks cool
bullets: The Edition XV headphone by HIFIMAN features a striking design and delivers a balanced sound profile, appealing to audiophiles.
With a focus on aesthetics and audio quality, the headphones are designed to provide a warm listening experience.
This product launch highlights the intersection of technology and design in consumer electronics, catering to discerning music lovers.

stories.title: NASA still confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the moon in 2028 despite spacesuit delays
bullets: NASA remains optimistic about landing astronauts on the moon by 2028, despite spacesuit delays reported on April 22, 2026.
The agency is addressing concerns over potential multiyear delays, emphasizing their commitment to the Artemis program's timeline.
Successful completion of the spacesuits is crucial for maintaining public and governmental support for lunar exploration.

stories.title: Mapping the environmental toll of the Middle East war
bullets: The ongoing US and Israel war on Iran has caused severe environmental damage, including oil refinery fires and pesticide factory strikes.
Reports indicate significant pollution in the Strait of Hormuz, raising public health concerns for local populations affected by the conflict.
This environmental toll may lead to international scrutiny and calls for accountability regarding wartime ecological impacts.

stories.title: ‘Pragmata’ review: ‘The Last Of Us’ meets ‘Alien’ in surprisingly wholesome space horror
bullets: The game 'Pragmata' blends elements from 'The Last Of Us' and 'Alien', featuring a cyborg and astronaut duo, reviewed on April 22, 2026.
Critics highlight the game's unique narrative and engaging gameplay, appealing to fans of both horror and adventure genres.
This release could set a new standard for storytelling in video games, influencing future titles in the industry.

stories.title: Prosecutors want 30 years for Syrian man accused of torture
bullets: Dutch prosecutors are seeking a 30-year prison sentence for a 57-year-old Syrian man accused of leading torture operations.
The man allegedly oversaw brutal interrogations during the Syrian civil war, with prosecutors citing numerous victim testimonies.
If convicted, this case could set a precedent for future war crime prosecutions in Europe.

stories.title: Ecco the Dolphin returns with new remaster, including brand-new game
bullets: Sega announced a remastered collection of 'Ecco the Dolphin' games, including a brand-new title, on April 22, 2026.
The collection aims to attract both nostalgic players and new fans, featuring updated graphics and gameplay mechanics.
This revival could reinvigorate interest in classic gaming franchises and expand Sega's market reach.

stories.title: Ecco the Dolphin: Complete announced, includes remasters of the first two entries and brand-new game
bullets: Ecco the Dolphin: Complete will feature remasters of the first two games and a brand-new title, set for release soon.
The project aims to revive interest in the franchise, appealing to both nostalgic fans and new players alike.
This release could reinvigorate the gaming community's interest in classic titles and their remakes.

stories.title: Trump Wants to Double Production of New Nuclear Weapon Cores
bullets: Trump's new budget proposal aims to double the production of nuclear weapon cores while cutting environmental cleanup funding.
The plan suggests reallocating $1.5 billion from environmental initiatives to support increased nuclear capabilities.
This shift raises concerns about national security priorities and environmental health among lawmakers and activists.

stories.title: Micro Aura Transgressions, With Giri Nathan
bullets: In a recent podcast episode, Giri Nathan discussed the NBA playoffs, focusing on the Knicks and Nuggets' Game 2 losses.
The conversation included humorous takes on player performances and rivalries, showcasing Nathan's basketball expertise.
This episode highlights the growing popularity of sports podcasts as platforms for in-depth analysis and fan engagement.

stories.title: A state-by-state look at the narrowing redistricting battle for the U.S. House
bullets: Virginia voters approved a constitutional amendment for a Democratic redistricting plan on April 21, 2026, impacting House elections.
This plan could potentially secure additional House seats for Democrats, reflecting shifting political dynamics in the state.
Florida is next in line for redistricting discussions, which could further influence the balance of power in Congress.

stories.title: Tricky announces new album ‘Different When It’s Silent’ with paranoid single ‘Out Of Place’ and summer UK and European tour dates
bullets: Tricky announced his new album 'Different When It's Silent' and the single 'Out Of Place' on April 22, 2026.
This marks Tricky's first solo album since 2020, featuring a paranoid theme and a summer tour across the UK and Europe.
The album's release and tour are significant for Tricky's career, potentially expanding his audience and influence in trip-hop.

stories.title: Smerz Reveal New Easy EP and Song “Spring Summer”
bullets: Norwegian duo Smerz revealed their new EP 'Easy' and single 'Spring Summer' on April 22, 2026, set for release next month.
The EP continues their exploration of postmodern pop, following their previous successful projects and collaborations.
This release is expected to enhance Smerz's reputation in the music industry, attracting more listeners and critical attention.

stories.title: The Fight to Save Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens
bullets: Gina Geffrard shared her memories of Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens during a podcast episode on April 22, 2026.
She recalled filming 'House Hunters' in 2019, where she discovered the gardens' significance and beauty across the street.
The podcast aims to raise awareness about the park's preservation, highlighting its cultural and environmental importance.

stories.title: Physicists think they've solved the muon mystery
bullets: Physicists have made significant advancements in understanding muons, confirming predictions of the Standard Model of particle physics.
Their findings provide precise measurements but eliminate the possibility of discovering a fifth fundamental force.
This research reinforces the Standard Model's validity, impacting future particle physics studies and experiments.

stories.title: Samsung engineer sentenced to 7 years in prison for selling chipmaking trade secrets to Chinese chipmaker — ex-employee supplied 10nm DRAM data to CXMT for $2 million
bullets: A former Samsung engineer was sentenced to 7 years in prison on April 22, 2026, for selling trade secrets to a Chinese firm.
He disclosed over 600 critical chipmaking steps and received $2 million for sharing 10nm DRAM data with CXMT.
This case highlights ongoing concerns about corporate espionage in the tech industry, prompting stricter security measures.

stories.title: Vibrant Sea Creatures Spring to Life in Lisa Stevens’ Textured Sculptures
bullets: Artist Lisa Stevens showcases vibrant sculptures inspired by underwater life, highlighting the beauty of marine organisms.
Her textured creations aim to raise awareness about ocean conservation and the importance of marine biodiversity.
Stevens' work contributes to the dialogue on environmental issues through the lens of contemporary art.

stories.title: Deals: TCL NXTPAPER 11/14 Android tabs from $180, Galaxy Tab S11 $320 off, Odyssey G7 monitor $504 off, more
bullets: TCL announced significant discounts on its NXTPAPER Android tablets and other devices on April 22, 2026.
Prices for the NXTPAPER tablets start at $160, marking a 30% reduction, while the Galaxy Tab S11 sees up to $320 off.
These deals are part of a broader strategy to boost sales and compete in the growing tablet market.

stories.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
bullets: Cybersecurity firm Symantec reported on April 22, 2026, that the Harvester group deployed a new Linux GoGra backdoor in South Asia.
The malware utilizes Microsoft Graph API for covert operations, posing a significant threat to targeted entities in the region.
This development underscores the evolving tactics of cybercriminals, necessitating enhanced cybersecurity measures for organizations.

stories.title: What the Explore-Exploit Dilemma Means for Athletes
bullets: The Explore-Exploit Dilemma examines athletes' decisions between traditional practices and new trends in sports.
Athletes face trade-offs, such as choosing between established training methods and innovative performance-enhancing products.
Understanding this dilemma can help athletes optimize their performance and adapt to evolving sports landscapes.

stories.title: Fifty days out, who is guaranteed to make Argentina’s World Cup squad?
bullets: Argentina's coach Lionel Scaloni is finalizing the World Cup squad, with key positions still under consideration as of April 22, 2026.
With the tournament approaching in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, Scaloni has identified most players but has spots still open.
This preparation is crucial for Argentina's strategy, aiming for a strong performance in the upcoming World Cup.

stories.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
bullets: Education Secretary Linda McMahon is leading efforts to dismantle the Department of Education under Trump's directive.
Her background in the wrestling industry informs her approach, focusing on privatization and reduced federal oversight.
This initiative could reshape the future of education policy and funding across the United States.

stories.title: A US Strategy For Defending Taiwan – Before a War
bullets: A strategic report outlines U.S. plans for defending Taiwan against potential military aggression from China.
The strategy emphasizes strengthening alliances and enhancing military readiness in the Asia-Pacific region.
This approach aims to deter aggression and maintain stability in a critical geopolitical area.

stories.title: Catherine Connolly signs International Protection Bill into law
bullets: Catherine Connolly has signed the International Protection Bill into law, enhancing protections for asylum seekers in Ireland.
The President opted not to challenge the legislation in the Supreme Court, allowing it to take effect immediately.
This law represents a significant step in Ireland's commitment to international human rights standards.

stories.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
bullets: Iranians are crossing into Turkey to access the internet, as Iran has cut off global internet access as of April 22, 2026.
Travelers report making brief visits solely for internet connectivity, highlighting severe restrictions on digital communication at home.
This trend underscores the growing demand for internet freedom and may prompt international responses to Iran's censorship.

stories.title: New court ruling blocks many of the government's anti-renewable policies
bullets: A recent court ruling has blocked several government policies that hinder the development of renewable energy projects.
The court found that the additional review processes targeting renewable initiatives lacked a solid legal foundation.
This decision could accelerate the transition to renewable energy sources and support climate change mitigation efforts.

stories.title: Control: Ultimate Edition is out for the iPhone and iPad
bullets: Control: Ultimate Edition launched on April 22, 2026, for iPhone and iPad, priced at $5.
This version includes the full game experience, compatible across iPhone, iPad, and Mac, enhancing accessibility.
The release aims to attract mobile gamers and expand the game's audience, following its success since 2019.

stories.title: Suicide deaths in U.S. teens and young adults fell after 988 launch
bullets: A study reveals that suicide deaths among U.S. teens and young adults decreased following the launch of the 988 Lifeline.
The report indicates a significant drop in suicide mortality rates, highlighting the effectiveness of the 988 service in providing support.
This decline underscores the importance of mental health resources, prompting calls for further investment in suicide prevention initiatives.

stories.title: Leaker: Apple to Downgrade iPhone 18 in Two Ways
bullets: Leaker reports that Apple plans to downgrade the iPhone 18's display and chip specifications to cut costs as of April 22, 2026.
The iPhone 18 will feature manufacturing downgrades aligning it with the lower-cost iPhone 18e model, affecting performance.
This strategy reflects Apple's response to market pressures and could influence consumer purchasing decisions.

stories.title: Lessons Learned Hacking Infra For 30 Years With Jon Brookes
bullets: Jon Brookes, a startup technical lead, shares insights from his 30-year career in hacking and infrastructure development.
He emphasizes the importance of digital sovereignty and open-source tools, advocating for user control over data outside Big Tech.
Brookes' vision includes leveraging AI for productivity, aiming to foster a collaborative online environment rather than one focused on growth.

stories.title: A Look Inside the HackerNoon Audience
bullets: HackerNoon reports over 6 million monthly readers, primarily technical professionals engaged in critical evaluation of content.
Readers spend an average of 6.4 minutes per article, with 72% reading most of what they open and 67% contributing to GitHub.
This engaged audience represents a significant market for tech insights, influencing trends and innovations in the industry.

stories.title: Governments failed to deliver $160m of river improvements including for now-parched NSW wetlands, report finds
bullets: An independent review reveals that NSW and Queensland governments failed to deliver over $160 million in river improvement projects by April 22, 2026.
The report criticizes the New South Wales government for not securing necessary private land access for floodplain improvements.
This underdelivery could exacerbate water management issues in the Murray-Darling basin, impacting agriculture and ecosystems.

stories.title: Podcast interviews of NSW couple jailed for abusing their daughter in the spotlight
bullets: Corrective Services NSW is investigating how journalist Richard Guilliatt interviewed convicted abusers Rob and Karen Gilfillan for a podcast.
The podcast, Shadow of Doubt, raised questions about the Gilfillans' guilt, which the victim claims has harmed her significantly.
This investigation highlights concerns over media access to convicted criminals and the impact on victims' mental health.

stories.title: As Iran talks go nowhere, will Donald Trump still go to Beijing?
bullets: US-Iran peace talks are stalled, casting doubt on Donald Trump's planned Beijing visit in early May 2026.
Trump extended a two-week ceasefire with Iran, announced just hours before its expiration, amid ongoing diplomatic tensions.
The outcome of these talks could influence international relations and Trump's foreign policy agenda in Asia.

stories.title: Increased calls to 988 hotline associated with a drop in suicides among young people, study finds
bullets: A new study shows an 11% drop in youth suicide rates following the launch of the 988 national suicide hotline in the U.S.
The hotline, designed for crisis intervention, has seen increased call volumes, indicating its growing importance for mental health support.
This data underscores the potential effectiveness of crisis hotlines in reducing suicide rates among vulnerable populations.

stories.title: STAT+: Gene therapy trial for deafness adds evidence to drug’s efficacy
bullets: A clinical trial in China found that 90% of participants improved hearing after receiving gene therapy for a rare deafness form.
This trial adds to the growing body of evidence supporting the efficacy of gene therapy in treating genetic hearing loss.
The findings could pave the way for broader applications of gene therapy in auditory health and related conditions.

stories.title: How climate change may increase antibiotic resistance
bullets: Research indicates that climate change may increase antibiotic resistance by promoting gene exchange among bacteria due to heat and drought.
This phenomenon poses significant risks to human health, potentially leading to more difficult-to-treat infections.
Understanding this link is crucial for developing strategies to combat antibiotic resistance in a changing climate.

stories.title: Egyptian mummy discovered stuffed with excerpt from ‘The Iliad’
bullets: Archaeologists discovered an Egyptian mummy containing an excerpt from Homer's Iliad, dating back 1,600 years.
The text, rather than magical spells, was used to help preserve the mummy's remains, showcasing ancient burial practices.
This find highlights the intersection of literature and archaeology, providing insights into cultural values of the time.

stories.title: A Powerful New ‘QR Code’ Untangles Math’s Knottiest Knots
bullets: Researchers have developed a new mathematical tool, likened to a QR code, to analyze complex knot structures.
This breakthrough aims to provide unprecedented insights into knot theory, potentially impacting various scientific fields.
The discovery could lead to advancements in understanding complex systems, with applications in physics and biology.

stories.title: The Race for the Next U.N. Chief Kicks Off
bullets: The selection process for the next U.N. Secretary-General begins, featuring four contenders facing rigorous questioning.
Current Secretary-General António Guterres's term is set to end, prompting discussions on the future direction of the U.N.
The outcome will influence global diplomacy and international relations, particularly in addressing pressing global issues.

stories.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
bullets: A compromised developer's repository is spreading malware through a new method called 'Contagious Interview' in North Korea.
This technique uses remote access Trojans (RATs) to infect systems, posing a significant cybersecurity threat.
The incident highlights the need for enhanced security measures and awareness to combat evolving cyber threats.

stories.title: LIVE UPDATES: ICC ruling on jurisdiction in Duterte’s drug war
bullets: The International Criminal Court is set to rule on its jurisdiction regarding Rodrigo Duterte's controversial drug war.
The ruling, expected today, could have significant implications for accountability in human rights violations in the Philippines.
This decision may influence international perceptions of Duterte's administration and its approach to law enforcement.

stories.title: At least 3 killed in Israeli strikes on south Lebanon, state media says, ahead of scheduled talks
bullets: Israeli airstrikes killed at least three individuals in southern Lebanon on April 22, escalating tensions ahead of planned ceasefire talks.
Hezbollah retaliated by launching an attack drone at Israeli forces, further complicating the fragile ceasefire situation.
The ongoing violence threatens to derail diplomatic efforts and could lead to a broader conflict in the region.

stories.title: 1 in 50 million split-colored lobster found in Massachusetts
bullets: A rare split-colored lobster, weighing three pounds, was discovered in Massachusetts, a phenomenon occurring once in 50 million times.
The lobster will be housed at an aquarium, providing educational opportunities about genetics and marine biology.
This unique find highlights the biodiversity of marine life and raises awareness about conservation efforts.

stories.title: Ukraine will get $105 billion loan after Hungary drops opposition, E.U. says
bullets: Ukraine will receive a $105 billion loan from the European Union after Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban withdrew his opposition.
Orban's decision followed his recent electoral defeat, which weakened his political stance against the loan.
This financial support is crucial for Ukraine's recovery and stability amid ongoing conflict and economic challenges.

stories.title: Why These Monkeys Are Eating Fistfuls of Dirt
bullets: A study reveals that monkeys are consuming dirt, raising questions about their dietary habits and environmental adaptations.
Researchers are investigating the nutritional benefits of soil consumption, which may provide essential minerals.
Understanding these behaviors could offer insights into animal diets and ecological interactions in their habitats.

stories.title: Where Is the China-Honduras Relationship Headed?
bullets: Honduras' new government shows no signs of improving relations with Taiwan, despite previous campaign promises.
Current diplomatic ties remain focused on China, raising concerns about regional geopolitical dynamics.
The situation could affect trade agreements and alliances in Central America, impacting U.S. interests in the region.

stories.title: 'The right thing for me right now' - Bethell responds to IPL stint criticism
bullets: Jacob Bethell addressed criticism regarding his limited playtime in the IPL, responding to calls from Sir Alastair Cook for a county cricket return.
Bethell, currently with the Kolkata Knight Riders, has played only two matches this season, raising concerns about his development.
His response highlights the tension between franchise commitments and player growth, especially for young talents in competitive leagues.

stories.title: With Midterms Coming Up, G.O.P. Is Bogged Down in Congress
bullets: Republican leaders in Congress are struggling to unify on key issues as midterm elections approach, creating legislative gridlock.
The Senate is resorting to extraordinary measures to pass homeland security funding amid a Democratic blockade.
This discord could impact the GOP's electoral prospects and influence future policy decisions leading up to the elections.

stories.title: Tehran Says It Has Seized Two Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Iranian Adviser Argues Cease-Fire Means 'Nothing'
bullets: Iranian forces seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz shortly after President Donald Trump announced a cease-fire extension.
An Iranian adviser dismissed the cease-fire's significance, indicating ongoing tensions in the region.
These actions could escalate maritime conflicts and impact global oil supply routes, raising international concerns.

stories.title: ICC upholds jurisdiction over Duterte case | The wRap
bullets: The International Criminal Court has upheld its jurisdiction over the case involving former President Rodrigo Duterte.
This decision reinforces the court's authority to investigate alleged human rights abuses during Duterte's drug war.
The ruling may lead to increased scrutiny of Duterte's policies and potential international legal actions against him.
